design_philosophy.core_loop: Drop colored capsules into a medicine bottle, rotate and position them to match 4-in-a-row with viruses of the same color; matches cause eliminations and cascade remaining pieces
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing falling piece placement and anticipating chain reactions while the bottle gradually fills; time pressure from falling speed and limited playfield space
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Dr. Mario treating viruses with medicinal capsules in a bottle; puzzle mastery through pattern recognition and spatial reasoning
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict Tetris-like falling block mechanics applied to a virus-elimination objective rather than pure line-clearing; emphasis on color-matching logic rather than shape tetromino fitting; includes chain-reaction cascades that reward planning depth; configurable difficulty through level, speed, and music selection provides player agency in challenge tuning

difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression; players can select starting level between 0 and 20
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players choose starting level (0-20), falling capsule speed (three options), and music before each game; higher levels increase virus count and complexity

progression.unlock_mechanism: Levels 0-20 with increasing virus counts; cutscenes appear after every 5th level completion (levels 5, 10, 15, 20) on Medium or High difficulty showing viruses in different locations
replayability_hooks: Configurable difficulty and speed settings enable multiple playstyles
Competitive multiplayer mode where chain reactions add capsules to opponent's field
Score-based progression with bonuses for multi-virus eliminations in single lines
Chain reaction mechanics reward discovery of advanced strategies

world_building_techniques: Thematic presentation: Dr. Mario uses medicine capsules to treat viruses in a bottle
Cutscenes between levels (every 5 levels) show anthropomorphic virus trio in different environmental scenes
Mario assumes doctor role; viruses portrayed as enemies to be eliminated
NES and Game Boy versions feature different cutscene locations, adding variety to world presentation

faction_indicators: Player vs. viruses (implicit conflict through elimination objective)
Multiplayer competitive framing: two-player mode pits players against each other with separate playing fields
danger_signals: Bottle filling to the neck creates immediate game-over threat
Cascading unmatched capsules signal loss of control
Opponent's chain reactions in multiplayer add hostile capsules to player's field
environmental_storytelling: Medicine bottle as contained laboratory setting for virus treatment
Capsule colors (red, yellow, blue) establish clear visual faction/virus identities
Cutscenes showing viruses in natural outdoor settings suggest they are creatures being displaced by treatment
Visual progression through levels via cutscene locations (tree, other environments) creates sense of spatial journey
